title: ASCII Sequence Control Blocks
problem: The ASCII sequence renderer currently rejects Mermaid sequence control blocks as endpoint-less control messages instead of rendering loop, opt, break, alt, par, and critical frames.
gates: cargo clippy -p merman-ascii -p merman --features ascii --all-targets -- -D warnings

evidence.result: opened the ASCII sequence control-block lane and selected executable boundary tests as the first implementation task
added executable inventory tests proving core control-signal line types, endpoint-less markers, labels, and current ASCII unsupported diagnostics
implemented ASCII and Unicode text frames for single-section loop, opt, and break sequence control blocks
implemented ASCII and Unicode sectioned frames for alt, par, and critical sequence control blocks while keeping rect and par_over deferred
covered nested and empty control-block diagnostics plus lifecycle, notes, and participant-box combinations
generated manual ASCII/Unicode control-block examples, passed final closeout gates, updated README/support evidence, and closed the lane

continue_policy.notes: Lane closed. Future work should be split around rect/par_over, nested control-block rendering, empty-section rendering, or exact Mermaid/SVG visual parity.
